title: ScholarEval evaluation (one proposal, one iteration)
description: The literature-grounded evaluation produced by roles/ScholarEval.md, faithful to arXiv 2510.16234: a Soundness module (per proposed method: Support / Contradictions / Suggestions) and a Contribution module (per contribution dimension: Strengths / Weaknesses / Suggestions). Every point cites real retrieved evidence by `cites` key so the Judge can apply its evidence gate. One file per iteration at <sandbox_root>/iter<N>/scholareval.json.

soundness.description: One entry per method extracted from the proposal's methodology/experiments.

method.description: The proposed methodological component being assessed.

support.description: Literature where similar applications of this method succeeded; why it transfers.

contradictions.description: Literature where this method (in similar context) failed or is limited; the risk it signals.

suggestions.description: Actionable fixes to raise the method's soundness.

contribution.description: One entry per contribution dimension extracted from the proposal (e.g. system design, data, evaluation methodology, conceptual framework).

dimension.description: The facet of contribution being compared against prior work.

strengths.description: Genuinely novel advances along this dimension vs. the compared papers.

weaknesses.description: Where the proposal's contribution along this dimension is already covered by prior work or is thin.

suggestions.description: Actionable ways to sharpen the novelty/significance along this dimension.

evidence.description: The citation pool. Every `cites` key in any point MUST resolve to an entry here, and every entry MUST come from a real tools/lit_search.py / WebFetch result actually retrieved this iteration — never from memory.

source.description: Which retrieval path produced it (s2 = lit_search search/snippet/cite; arxiv = fulltext; web = WebSearch/WebFetch fallback).

id.description: Semantic Scholar paperId, arXiv id, or URL.

snippet.description: The exact retrieved passage that backs claims citing this key. Verbatim, not paraphrased.

cites.description: Keys into `evidence`. An empty array means UNGROUNDED — the Judge discounts it.
